title: Intelligence FilterSet (report_type ∈ {1, 2, 3})
description: Schema for the FilterSet payload of CONTENT (1), BRANDS (2), and THOUGHTLEADERS/CHANNELS (3) reports. Field names and types mirror dashboard.models.FilterSet 1:1 — no invented fields. Phase 2 emits a JSON object that conforms to this schema.

report_type_meanings.1: CONTENT — videos / articles / uploads (ES content index)

_tl_source_of_truth.note: All three types share the same FilterSet model. Field relevance differs per type — see each field's _tl_intent_hints and _tl_default_by_report_type, plus the columns_<type>.md and _tl_intent_overrides for intent-driven population.

keywords.description: Free-text keywords. Each entry is a phrase that will be ILIKE-matched against the fields listed in `content_fields` (or the per-keyword override in `keyword_content_fields_map`).
_tl_intent_hints: Multi-word phrases stay as a single array entry — they will match as a substring, not be tokenized.
Use sub-segment terms when the head term is noisy (e.g. ['printful t-shirt', 'print on demand store'] instead of just ['print']).

keyword_operator.description: How multiple `keywords` entries combine. AND = every keyword must match; OR = any.
_tl_intent_hints: Use AND when the user explicitly wants all terms present (e.g. 'channels that talk about both X AND Y').
Use OR for synonym sets ('printful OR teespring OR print on demand').

content_fields.description: Default fields against which `keywords` are matched. Overridable per-keyword via `keyword_content_fields_map`.

keyword_content_fields_map.description: Per-keyword content_fields override. Keys are stringified positions in the `keywords` array ('0', '1', ...); values are arrays of ContentField values.
keyword_content_fields_map._tl_emit_only_when: A specific keyword needs a different match surface than the default `content_fields`.

keyword_exclude_map.description: Per-keyword exclude flag. Keys are stringified positions in the `keywords` array; values are booleans. Excluded keywords are NOT-matched (substring negation).
keyword_exclude_map._tl_emit_only_when: User says 'but not X' / 'exclude Y' — encode as a keyword entry plus an exclude flag at the same position.

topics.description: Topic IDs from `thoughtleaders_topics`. Used to anchor a report to one or more curated topic clusters.
_tl_intent_hints: Phase 2 may emit topic IDs when the topic_matcher tool returns at least one strong match.
When emitted, downstream still expands the topic's curated keywords[] into `keywords`/`content_fields` — `topics` is not a self-sufficient filter.

channel_formats.description: Restrict to specific channel formats. 1=Newsletter, 3=Podcast, 4=Video, 5=Blog post, 7=Twitch, 8=TikTok, 9=Instagram, 10=LinkedIn.
channel_formats._tl_default_meaning: YouTube Video — current TL focus is YouTube only.

content_categories.description: Include channels whose content_category matches any of these IDs.

content_types.description: Free-string array of ES content_type values.

languages.description: ISO language codes (e.g. 'en', 'es'). Resolved against YoutubeLanguages.get_language_list().

days_ago.description: Rolling lower bound: only items from the last N days.

days_ago_to.description: Rolling upper bound: items at most N days old.

sort.description: Sortable column key from references/sortable_columns.json. Format: 'field' (asc) or '-field' (desc).

_tl_intent_hints: Set to 'exists' when the user's intent is outreach (e.g. 'find me channels we can email').

demographic_age.description: Age-bucket distribution constraint. Shape preserved as JSONField from the platform.

reach_from.description: Channel reach lower bound. Reach is TL's rolling audience-size metric (not raw subs).
_tl_intent_hints: Set when user gives a size signal ('mid-size channels', 'big channels', '50k–500k reach').

is_offline.description: Stored as a short string in the model — preserve the platform's encoding rather than transforming.

tl_sponsorships_only.description: Restrict to channels TL has sponsorship history with.

performance_grade.description: TL's internal performance grade buckets. Numeric IDs as stored.

msn_channels_only.description: Restrict to MSN (Media Selling Network) channels only.

filters_json.description: Catch-all JSONField the platform interprets directly. Use for filter signals that don't have a first-class column. Phase 2 should prefer a typed field whenever one exists.
filters_json._tl_emit_only_when: No first-class field maps to the user's intent.

channels.description: Resolved channel IDs (M2M). The skill resolves channel names → IDs via the name_resolver tool before emitting.

_tl_rules: Do NOT invent fields. If the user's intent has no matching field, encode it in `filters_json` (last resort) or surface it as a clarifying question.
When emitting a date range, prefer days_ago/days_ago_to for rolling intent and start_date/end_date for absolute intent.
Always set `languages: ['en']` and `channel_formats: [4]` (YouTube Video) unless the user explicitly contradicts them — these are TL's working defaults.
Resolve channel/brand names → IDs via the name_resolver tool BEFORE emitting `channels`/`brands` arrays.
When `keyword_operator` is unset and `keywords` has more than one entry, downstream defaults to OR — set it explicitly to AND if intent calls for it.
Keep `keywords` tight — better to ship 3 high-precision phrases than 12 noisy ones (Phase 3 sample_judge will catch noise either way, but tighter is faster).
